bead.title: checks: residual production-reachability — internal/registry installer
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Current deadcode RTA on 2026-05-15 still reports `internal/registry.InstallPackageFromDir` as unreachable from the production graph, so `ddx-83440482` cannot close from current-tree evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`.ddx/executions/20260515T210515-1a20052a/production-reachability-final.json` reports `cli/internal/registry/installer.go:65` — `InstallPackageFromDir`.

PROPOSED FIX
Resolve the symbol currently listed for `cli/internal/registry/installer.go` in the committed artifact. WIRE it if a production caller should still exist; DELETE it if the installer surface has moved on to different entry points. If deferred investigation is still needed after ~15 minutes, annotate it with `// wiring:pending <follow-up-bead-id>` and file the narrower follow-up before closing this bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not touch artifact-type residuals tracked separately.
- Do not change the production-reachability protocol or checker implementation.
bead.acceptance:
1. Every symbol currently listed for this cluster in `.ddx/executions/20260515T210515-1a20052a/production-reachability-final.json` is either wired into the production graph or deleted.
2. `cd cli && go run golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@v0.42.0 ./... | rg 'internal/registry/installer\.go'` returns no hits.
3. Any remaining `// wiring:pending` annotation created while resolving this cluster cites an open follow-up bead.
4. Decisions log written to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/decisions.md` records WIRE|DELETE|PENDING for each listed symbol.
5. `cd cli && go test ./...` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
